sidebar.rememberNote: Cover the screen. Answer out loud or on paper before you click reveal. Recognition lies; retrieval is the real test.

home.title: Final Exam Prep
home.subtitle: Three subjects · handwritten exam · active-recall study sheets
home.methodTitle: How to use these sheets (the method that fixes recall)
home.step1: Read the concept box first. Each topic opens with the mental model: the why, so facts stick.
home.step2: Then close it and retrieve. Each drill shows a question; the answer is hidden. Say or write your answer before clicking reveal. The struggle to recall is what builds memory; reading the answer does almost nothing.
home.step3: Mark misses, repeat tomorrow. The ones you fail are your real study list. Re-test them, don't re-read them.
home.step4: Write the configs/code by hand. Your exam is handwritten, so practice writing OSPF/Intent/Dart from a blank page, not just recognising them.
home.tip: Spacing beats cramming: three 40-minute retrieval sessions across three days beats one long read the night before. Do one subject per day on rotation, then full mixed review in the last 3 days.
home.langBody: Use the language switcher in the sidebar to read every sheet in English or Khmer. Hit Print / PDF to export a print-ready copy (answers auto-revealed) for offline / handwriting practice.
home.sourcesIntro: These review sheets were written from our three course documents. Each subject is a condensed, beginner-friendly retelling of its source:

countdown.today: Exam is today. You've got this.
